skill.name: stitch-design
skill.description: Transforms text descriptions into professional UI designs using Stitch MCP. Enhances prompts with UI/UX terminology, maintains design system consistency, and generates high-fidelity screens.
skill.summary: AI-powered UI design skill that converts text descriptions into polished designs using Stitch MCP server.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: All 213 static findings evaluated as false positives. Hex color codes misidentified as cryptographic algorithms, markdown code blocks and CSS class names misidentified as shell execution, and documentation paths misidentified as path traversal. No malicious code patterns confirmed. Skill is a legitimate design system tool for Stitch MCP.

content.user_title: Create professional UI designs from text prompts
content.value_statement: Design teams spend hours creating UI mockups manually. The Stitch Design skill automates high-fidelity screen generation from text descriptions while maintaining design system consistency.
actual_capabilities: Generate high-fidelity UI screens from natural language descriptions
Enhance vague design requests with professional UI/UX terminology
Create and maintain project design systems in .stitch/DESIGN.md format
Edit existing screens with targeted design changes
Download generated HTML and screenshots locally for development
Ensure design consistency across screens using established visual language
limitations: Requires Stitch MCP server access and project credentials
Design quality depends on prompt specificity and clarity
Cannot implement interactive frontend code, only generates visual designs
Design system analysis requires existing screens to reference

use_cases.title: Rapid prototyping for startups
use_cases.description: Startup founders generate landing pages and feature screens in minutes instead of hours, iterating quickly on design direction.

use_cases.title: Design system documentation
use_cases.description: Design leads extract and document the visual language from existing Stitch projects into consistent .stitch/DESIGN.md files.

use_cases.title: Cross-functional design handoff
use_cases.description: Product managers describe desired screens and designers refine the AI-generated outputs, streamlining collaboration.

prompt_templates.title: Basic screen generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a landing page for [product name]. Focus on [key feature]. Use a [modern/minimal/professional] aesthetic with [color preference if any].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick initial mockup

prompt_templates.title: Design system-aware generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a [page type] that matches our existing design system in .stitch/DESIGN.md. Include [specific components] with emphasis on [visual priority].

prompt_templates.title: Targeted screen edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the [specific screen] in our [project name] project. Change the [specific element] to [new specification]. Keep everything else consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Design system extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze our Stitch project [project name/ID] and create a comprehensive .stitch/DESIGN.md documenting our visual language, color palette, typography, and component patterns.

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a meditation app with a calming, serene aesthetic.
output_examples.output: A professional landing page featuring a hero section with nature imagery, centered logo, and primary CTA button. Color palette uses Deep Ocean Blue (#1a365d) for CTAs, Calm Slate (#718096) for secondary elements, and Soft Mist (#f7fafc) for background. Typography uses Montserrat for headings and Inter for body text.

output_examples.input: Create a mobile app dashboard showing user stats and daily goals progress.
output_examples.output: A clean mobile-first dashboard layout with a sticky header showing the app logo and navigation. Main content area displays stat cards with rounded corners (12px), subtle elevation shadows, and the brand accent color for progress indicators. Footer contains quick action buttons.

best_practices: Be specific about visual elements: name exact colors using hex codes, describe shapes and shadows in natural language, and define the overall mood or atmosphere.
Reference your design system: check for .stitch/DESIGN.md and incorporate existing tokens before generating new screens to maintain visual consistency.
Iterate with edits: use targeted edit prompts for refinements rather than regenerating entire screens, preserving the work already completed.
anti_patterns: Avoid vague requests like 'make it look nice' or 'add a good button'. Be explicit about the component type, color role, and interaction behavior you want.
Do not ignore generated suggestions: the skill provides text descriptions and improvement suggestions in outputComponents that help refine future iterations.
Avoid mixing design systems: do not reference tokens from different visual languages in a single prompt, as this creates inconsistent output.

faq.question: What is Stitch MCP and do I need an account?
faq.answer: Stitch MCP is a model context protocol server for design generation. You need access to a Stitch project and project ID to use this skill effectively.

faq.question: Can this skill write actual code?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates visual designs as HTML and screenshots. It does not write frontend code like React or Vue components.

faq.question: How does the design system extraction work?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes existing screens from your Stitch project, extracts color codes, typography rules, and component styles, then synthesizes them into a .stitch/DESIGN.md documentation file.

faq.question: What device types are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports DESKTOP, MOBILE, and TABLET device types when generating screens through the Stitch MCP generate_screen_from_text tool.

faq.question: How do I refine a generated design?
faq.answer: Use the edit-design workflow with a specific, targeted prompt like 'change the hero background to white and make the CTA button 20% larger' rather than regenerating from scratch.

faq.question: Can I use this with other skills?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill integrates with react:components for handing off to frontend implementation and stitch-loop for providing design context during autonomous building workflows.
